message:
Audit and enhance UX beads with integration guidance

- Reopened T6 (empty states) and T9 (truncation standardization) after
  discovering they have genuine incremental value beyond existing code
- Added "Build-On-Existing Infrastructure" notes to all beads identifying
  what already exists and what's truly new
- Added AI Agent Integration Guidance to all R-series beads with:
  - Self-documenting JSON output patterns
  - _agent_hints sections for programmatic parsing
  - Concrete Python/Go code examples
  - Exit code semantics (0=success, 1=error, 2=unavailable)
- Verified dependency structure is correct:
  - T2 → T1 (help bar)
  - T7 → T6 (empty states)
  - R4, R9 → R2 (error responses)
  - R8 → R6, R7 (timestamps, semantics)

Key findings from codebase audit:
- 21+ places still use "..." instead of "…" (T9 addresses)
- RenderHelpBar exists but dashboard doesn't use it (T1 addresses)
- EmptyState component exists but lacks semantic icons (T6 extends)
- ScrollIndicator exists but not wired to panels (T3 addresses)
- RFC3339 widely used but needs audit for consistency (R6 addresses)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
